scope_note_changelog.change: Initial PEEL prompt bank per VOCAB_COACH_PROMPT_BANK_GENERATION_PROMPT_V1.md. 228 prompt entries covering all 6 flat topics and all 16 real subtopics (across environment/technology/health/education) x all 4 task types (opinion, advantages_disadvantages, cause_effect_problem_solution, discussion), at least 2 prompts per (topic-or-subtopic x task_type) combination -- most units carry 10 prompts (2 opinion + 2 advantages_disadvantages + 4 cause_effect_problem_solution, covering all 4 angles, + 2 discussion). 4 additional multi-subtopic worked examples (one pair per subtopic-bearing topic: environment::energy_and_resources+pollution, technology::devices_and_innovation+data_and_security, health::lifestyle_and_prevention+mental_health, education::skills_and_curriculum+access_and_equality), each authored as 2 entries (advantage/disadvantage) sharing one scenario_text, drawing vocabulary from the union of both tagged subtopics. Every suggested_vocabulary item is copied verbatim from vocab_coach_topic_bank_v1_3_0.json or vocab_coach_task_type_bank_v1_2_0.json and re-verified by this build script against those live files (see verification section of the addendum) -- none invented or paraphrased. Every instruction_template has an explicit singular-angle marker ('ONE advantage', 'ONE cause', etc.) baked into the wording itself. No CEFR field added to prompt entries (band-gating stays on vocabulary items only, per Requirement 6). Correction to this prompt's own Requirement 8: it asserted 'no vocab_coach_engine*.py or equivalent exists anywhere in the project' -- this is factually incorrect. vocab_coach_engine_v1_0_0.py through vocab_coach_engine_v1_3_0.py already exist in gold/LRET/vocab_coach/, built earlier in this same working session. The substance of Requirement 8 still holds and was honoured: none of those engine files currently read this prompt bank, the topic bank's subtopics/angle-aware selection, or the task-type bank's angle field (a gap already flagged in VOCAB_COACH_BANK_REVISION_V1_addendum.md). This is stated here as 'when an engine is updated to consume this bank, it will need to...', not as though a currently-reading engine exists and would break.

Per VOCAB_COACH_PROMPT_BANK_EXTENSION_PROMPT_V2.md: closed all 35 prompt-bank coverage gaps created by vocab_coach_topic_bank_v1_4_0.json's 8 new topics (society_and_ageing, family_and_relationships, globalization_and_international_relations, sport_and_leisure, science_and_space, food_and_diet, transportation, language_and_culture -- 23 regular subtopics + 8 academic_collocations subtopics = 31 units) PLUS the academic_collocations gap that already silently existed for the 4 ORIGINAL subtopic-bearing topics (environment, technology, health, education -- 4 units), for 35 total, matching the real gap re-derived directly from vocab_coach_selection_engine_v1_0.py's actual enumerate_units() logic against vocab_coach_topic_bank_v1_4_0.json (57 total rotation units, 22 pre-existing + 35 gap -- re-derived rather than trusted from the extension prompt's own count, per its own verification checklist item 1). Added 350 gap-closing prompts (10 per unit: 1 prompt per task_type/angle combo x 8 combos + 2 opinion prompts, reusing 2 authored scenarios per unit -- the density pattern v1_0_0 ACTUALLY used, verified directly against the real file; see known_limitations for a correction to the extension prompt's differing claim) + 16 multi-subtopic bonus prompts (1 genuine 2-subtopic advantage/disadvantage pair per new topic, per requirement 4). All 228 original prompts copied through byte-identical. New total: 594 prompts.

known_limitations: Per unit, only 2 concrete scenario_texts (A and B) are authored and reused across the unit's 10 task-type/angle prompt entries. This keeps the bank internally consistent and dedup-clean ((scenario_text, task_type, angle) is always unique) but means a student who does many sessions on the same topic will eventually see a repeated scenario paired with a new angle/task_type -- this is a deliberate authoring-scale tradeoff, not an oversight, and more scenario variety per unit is a natural v1.1.0 expansion if session volume warrants it.

Task-type vocabulary (opinion/cause-effect/advantages-disadvantages/discussion phrases) is drawn from small rotating pools shared across all 22 units, since task-type phrases are inherently topic-agnostic. The same rhetorical phrase (e.g. 'result in') can appear in suggested_vocabulary for more than one prompt -- this does not violate the dedup rule (which is keyed on scenario_text + task_type + angle, not vocabulary contents) and mirrors how the task-type bank itself is meant to be reused across topics.

The 4 multi-subtopic worked examples pool vocabulary from exactly 2 subtopics within the same topic (not cross-topic combinations, e.g. environment+urbanization), per the spec's own example phrasing ('two subtopics within one topic'). Cross-topic scenario pairing was not attempted in this pass.

The extension prompt (VOCAB_COACH_PROMPT_BANK_EXTENSION_PROMPT_V2.md) asserts new units should get '2-3 prompts per (unit x task_type) combination... same bar v1_0_0 used' and its own verification checklist asks to confirm '4 for opinion.' Neither matches the real v1_0_0 file: every one of its 22 single-subtopic units has EXACTLY 1 prompt per task_type/angle combo (8 combos) plus 2 opinion prompts = 10 prompts/unit, confirmed by direct inspection, not estimated. This build follows the REAL v1_0_0 precedent (10 prompts/unit, 2 authored scenarios reused across combos) for consistency with the existing 228 prompts, rather than the extension prompt's differing (and unverified) claim about what v1_0_0 did.

This build also found that vocab_coach_topic_bank_v1_5_0.json, vocab_coach_selection_engine_v1_1.py, vocab_coach_selection_engine_v1_2.py, and Academic_Words_Redesign_Spec_v1.docx -- all referenced by the extension prompt as already-existing prerequisites -- do not exist anywhere in this project. The only real files are vocab_coach_topic_bank_v1_4_0.json and vocab_coach_selection_engine_v1_0.py. Verified this doesn't block the actual gap-closing work: v1_0.py's enumerate_units()/filter_candidates() already implement exactly the coverage-gap crash the extension prompt describes (same SystemExit message), and the extension prompt's own claim that v1_5_0 'did not add or remove any topics/subtopics' means v1_4_0 is structurally sufficient for gap computation. The 35-unit / 57-total-unit counts were independently re-derived against v1_4_0 + v1_0.py and matched exactly.

Vocabulary selection for the new prompts was done programmatically (rotating through each unit's real item pool) rather than hand-picked per prompt the way v1_0_0's original 228 were -- every phrase is still a real, verbatim entry from the actual topic/task-type bank files (by construction, not by spot-check alone), but the specific combinations were not individually curated for maximum topical fit the way a human author would. Spot-checked in verification (see addendum) rather than exhaustively reviewed.

Some of the 35 gap units draw from genuinely small item pools (e.g. several academic_collocations subtopics have only 4-7 items) -- vocabulary picks for that unit's 10 prompts necessarily overlap more than in a larger subtopic. Documented rather than hidden; this mirrors the same honest-disclosure precedent set for e.g. environment::waste_and_recycling in v1.3.0/v1.4.0 of the topic bank.
